title: Digital Signatures with Ed25519 and ECDSA · Execution Trace: Crypto Go Signature Policy
summary: A package signs human-readable JSON, accepts keys from the message being verified, ignores signature context, and reports that signatures prove truth and non-repudiation. Predict the asset, adversary, security property, trust boundary, and falsifying evidence before opening the governing standard. Planned artifact: Add and verify the signature policy increment in versioned signed artifact verifier. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, a…
objectives: Apply threshold, authorization, revocation, expiry, transparency, and audit policy after cryptographic verification.

steps.title: Cryptography and Go Predict Crypto Go Signature Policy · Theory Crypto Go Signature Policy
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory cryptographic engineering team handles case 9300 during a replicated device restart. Build deterministic Go evidence to verify canonical context-bound Ed25519 or ECDSA bytes under a trusted key and separate authorization policy; retain exact canonical bytes, purpose, role, version, and artifact identity; then remove one transfer gate and state exactly which production claim is no longer supported. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Crypto Go Signature Policy contract for the supplied data.

content.title: Worked case pattern

Build deterministic Go evidence to verify canonical context-bound Ed25519 or ECDSA bytes under a trusted key and separate authorization policy; retain exact canonical bytes, purpose, role, version, and artifact identity; then remove one transfer gate and state exactly which production claim is no longer supported. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Apply threshold, authorization, revocation, expiry, transparency, and audit policy after cryptographic verification. A defensible solution applies Crypto Go Signature Policy, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to versioned signed artifact verifier.

content.code:
package main

import (
	"crypto/ed25519"
	"fmt"
)

func main() {
	seed := make([]byte, ed25519.SeedSize)
	seed[0] = 4
	privateKey := ed25519.NewKeyFromSeed(seed)
	publicKey := privateKey.Public().(ed25519.PublicKey)
	message := []byte("update-manifest|v1|artifact-4")
	signature := ed25519.Sign(privateKey, message)
	valid := ed25519.Verify(publicKey, message, signature)
	changed := ed25519.Verify(publicKey, append(message, '!'), signature)
	fmt.Printf("valid=%t changed_valid=%t trust_policy_required=true fixture_key_only=true\n", valid, changed)
}
// Worked focus: crypto-go-signature-policy — Apply threshold, authorization, revocation, expiry, transparency, and audit policy after cryptographic verification.
